message:
feat(core): implement PRAGMA table_info, enrich ColumnInfo, fix HAVING NULL semantics

This change spans several crates to add PRAGMA table_info support and
fix expression evaluation correctness:

ColumnInfo metadata (codegen, compat_persist, connection): ColumnInfo
now carries type_name, notnull, and default_value fields. These are
populated from the AST during CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN,
enabling introspection queries. A ColumnInfo::basic() constructor is
added for call sites that do not need the extended metadata.

PRAGMA table_info/table_xinfo (connection): Implements the standard
6-column result set (cid, name, type, notnull, dflt_value, pk) by
reading from the in-memory schema. A format_default_value helper
serializes DefaultValue AST nodes back to SQL text.

HAVING clause fixes (connection): Comparison operators (=, !=, <, >,
<=, >=) now propagate NULL per SQL standard — any comparison with NULL
yields NULL, not 0. AND/OR use three-valued logic: AND returns 0 if
either operand is definitively false even when the other is NULL; OR
returns 1 if either operand is truthy even when the other is NULL.
Also adds divide, modulo, and concat operators to the HAVING evaluator,
which were previously falling through to the default NULL case.

group_concat separator (agg_builtins): The separator argument is now
applied on every step call, not just the first. While the separator is
typically a constant, applying it consistently avoids subtle bugs if
the expression varies across rows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -459,6 +459,9 @@ pub fn parse_columns_from_create_sql(sql: &str) -> Vec<ColumnInfo> {
                 name,
                 affinity,
                 is_ipk,
+                type_name: if type_decl.is_empty() { None } else { Some(type_decl) },
+                notnull: upper.contains("NOT NULL"),
+                default_value: None,
             })
         })
         .collect()
